title: Do vs Make
explanation:
Do vs Make

Do — activities, jobs, tasks: do homework, do the shopping, do your best, do exercise

Make — create/produce: make a cake, make a decision, make money, make a mistake, make friends

Learn common collocations — they don't always follow a rule!

questions.text: They [*made|did|make] a lot of friends at university.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Can you ____ me a favour? | *do | make | did
She ____ a lot of money in her job. | *makes | does | make
I ____ yoga twice a week. | *do | make | did
He ____ a phone call to his boss. | *made | did | make
We [*did|made|do] our best to finish on time.
She [*made|did|make] a promise she couldn't keep.
